gaps: No test case verifies that the correct product name (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20', 'Poly Sync 20+') is displayed in Device Settings or Onboarding screens instead of the internal device code name (e.g., 'Falcon').
No test case validates that device metadata (product name vs. codename) is correctly mapped and displayed across the app UI, specifically in Device Settings and Onboarding flows.
No test case covers the scenario where a device's internal Bluetooth codename leaks into user-facing UI elements like onboarding screens or device settings headers.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-001: Verify Product Name Displays Correctly on Onboarding Screen for Sync 20
steps: Factory reset the Poly Sync 20 speakerphone (press and hold Power + Bluetooth buttons for 5 seconds until LCD flashes red).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Ensure Bluetooth is enabled on the phone.
Press the Bluetooth button on the Sync 20 for 2 seconds until you hear 'pairing' and LCD flashes blue.
In the app, tap 'Add Device' or wait for the device discovery prompt.
When the Sync 20 appears in the discovered devices list, tap it to initiate pairing.
Observe the onboarding screen that appears after successful pairing (e.g., 'Device Connected' or 'Set Up Your Device' screen).
Note the product name displayed in the header/title of the onboarding screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The onboarding screen displays the proper product name 'Poly Sync 20' (or 'Poly Sync 20+'). The internal code name 'Falcon' is NOT shown anywhere on the onboarding screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-002: Verify Product Name Displays Correctly on Onboarding Screen for Sync 20+
steps: Factory reset the Poly Sync 20+ speakerphone (press and hold Power + Bluetooth buttons for 5 seconds until LCD flashes red).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app on Android.
Ensure Bluetooth is enabled on the phone.
Press the Bluetooth button on the Sync 20+ for 2 seconds until you hear 'pairing' and LCD flashes blue.
In the app, tap 'Add Device' or wait for the device discovery prompt.
When the Sync 20+ appears in the discovered devices list, tap it to initiate pairing.
Observe the onboarding screen that appears after successful pairing.
Note the product name displayed in the header/title of the onboarding screen.
generated_testcases.expected: The onboarding screen displays the proper product name 'Poly Sync 20+'. The internal code name 'Falcon' is NOT shown anywhere on the onboarding screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-001: Verify Product Name in Device Settings Header for Sync 20
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected Sync 20 device card or name to open Device Settings.
Observe the header/title area at the top of the Device Settings screen (e.g., the device name displayed next to the device icon or in the top bar).
Note the exact text displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: The Device Settings screen header displays the proper product name 'Poly Sync 20'. The internal code name 'Falcon' is NOT shown in the header, title, or any user-facing label on this screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-002: Verify Product Name in Device Settings Header for Sync 20+
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20+ speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the connected Sync 20+ device card or name to open Device Settings.
Observe the header/title area at the top of the Device Settings screen.
Note the exact text displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: The Device Settings screen header displays the proper product name 'Poly Sync 20+'. The internal code name 'Falcon' is NOT shown in the header, title, or any user-facing label on this screen.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-003: Verify Product Name Persists After App Restart
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Device Settings and verify the header shows 'Poly Sync 20'.
Press the Home button to background the app.
Fully close (swipe away) the Poly Studio Mobile app from the recent apps list.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Wait for the app to reconnect to the Sync 20 automatically.
Tap the device card to open Device Settings.
Observe the header/title area.
generated_testcases.expected: After app restart and reconnection, the Device Settings header still displays 'Poly Sync 20' (not 'Falcon').

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-004: Verify Product Name Persists After Bluetooth Disconnect/Reconnect
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Device Settings and verify the header shows 'Poly Sync 20'.
On the phone, go to Bluetooth settings and tap 'Forget' or 'Unpair' the Sync 20.
Wait 5 seconds.
Re-pair the Sync 20 by pressing the Bluetooth button for 2 seconds and selecting it in phone Bluetooth settings.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
Tap the reconnected Sync 20 device card to open Device Settings.
Observe the header/title area.
generated_testcases.expected: After Bluetooth disconnect and re-pairing, the Device Settings header displays 'Poly Sync 20' (not 'Falcon').

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-005: Verify Product Name Persists After Device Power Cycle
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Device Settings and verify the header shows 'Poly Sync 20'.
Press the Power button on the Sync 20 for 2 seconds to power it off (LCD turns off).
Wait 10 seconds.
Press the Power button on the Sync 20 for 2 seconds to power it back on.
Wait for the app to automatically reconnect (up to 30 seconds).
Tap the device card to open Device Settings.
Observe the header/title area.
generated_testcases.expected: After device power off/on cycle and reconnection, the Device Settings header displays 'Poly Sync 20' (not 'Falcon').

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-003: Verify Product Name on Onboarding After App Force-Kill
steps: Factory reset the Poly Sync 20 speakerphone.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and begin the pairing process.
When the Sync 20 appears in discovered devices, tap it to start pairing.
During the onboarding screen display (before completing setup), press the Home button.
Fully close (swipe away) the Poly Studio Mobile app from recent apps.
Reopen Poly Studio Mobile app.
Observe the screen that appears (may show the device as connected or prompt to finish setup).
If the device is shown, tap it to view details or complete onboarding.
Note the product name displayed.
generated_testcases.expected: After app force-kill during onboarding and relaunch, the product name displayed is 'Poly Sync 20' (not 'Falcon').

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-006: Verify Product Name in Audio Settings Sub-Screen
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Open Device Settings by tapping the device card.
Tap 'Audio Settings' or the audio settings option.
Observe the title/header at the top of the Audio Settings screen.
Navigate to sub-screens: tap 'Transparency Mode', then 'Environment', then 'Speech'.
At each sub-screen, observe the header/title area.
generated_testcases.expected: The Audio Settings screen and all sub-screens (Transparency Mode, Environment, Speech) display 'Poly Sync 20' in the header/title area. The code name 'Falcon' is not shown on any of these screens.

generated_testcases.title: TC-DEVSETTINGS-007: Verify Product Name for Multiple Paired Devices (Sync 20 and Sync 20+)
steps: Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20 speakerphone to the Poly Studio Mobile app.
Pair and connect a Poly Sync 20+ speakerphone to the same phone (the app should show both devices).
Open Poly Studio Mobile app.
In the device list, observe the name displayed for each device.
Tap the Sync 20 device card to open its Device Settings and note the header.
Go back to the device list.
Tap the Sync 20+ device card to open its Device Settings and note the header.
generated_testcases.expected: The Sync 20 device shows 'Poly Sync 20' and the Sync 20+ device shows 'Poly Sync 20+' in both the device list and Device Settings headers. Neither shows 'Falcon'.

generated_testcases.title: TC-ONBOARD-004: Verify Product Name on Onboarding Screen for All Supported Device Types
steps: Identify all Poly devices supported by the Poly Studio Mobile app (e.g., Sync 20, Sync 20+, Sync 40, Sync 60, Voyager series, etc.).
For each device type, perform a factory reset.
Open Poly Studio Mobile app and initiate pairing.
Complete the pairing process and observe the onboarding screen.
Note the product name displayed for each device.
generated_testcases.expected: Each device displays its proper commercial product name on the onboarding screen (e.g., 'Poly Sync 20', 'Poly Sync 20+', 'Poly Sync 40', etc.). No device shows its internal code name in any user-facing UI element.
